title: Northeast San Antonio
name: Northeast San Antonio
metaTitle: Northeast San Antonio 1031 Exchange Replacement Properties
metaDescription: 1031 exchange coordination for retail and industrial property in Northeast San Antonio along the I-35 corridor, near Randolph Air Force Base and the Judson area.

sections.html: Northeast San Antonio runs along the I-35 corridor toward New Braunfels, with Randolph Air Force Base and the surrounding Judson and Converse communities anchoring a mix of retail, light industrial, and logistics activity. It's a working corridor shaped as much by military and industrial employment as by residential retail demand, which gives 1031 exchange buyers a somewhat different asset mix than the more retail-heavy North Side.

sections.heading: Retail and Industrial Stock Along I-35 and Northeast Corridors
sections.html:
Retail here concentrates around FM 78 and Loop 410's northeast quadrant, serving the residential population around Randolph AFB and the Judson and Converse areas with grocery-anchored centers and national quick-service and convenience tenants. Light industrial and flex space runs along I-35 itself, benefiting from direct highway access toward both San Antonio's urban core and the Austin corridor to the north.

Randolph AFB's presence supports steady demand for retail and service tenants catering to military families and base personnel, a demand driver that behaves differently from the discretionary spending patterns found in more affluent North Side submarkets.

sections.heading: What Drives Demand in Northeast San Antonio
sections.html: Randolph Air Force Base provides a stable, recession-resistant population base that supports nearby retail and service tenants regardless of broader economic conditions. The I-35 corridor's direct connection between San Antonio and Austin also continues to draw logistics and light industrial tenants seeking to serve both metros from a single location, while continued residential growth in Converse and the surrounding area supports steady rooftop growth for retail trade areas.

sections.heading: Closing Coordination for a Northeast San Antonio Property
sections.html:
Properties near Randolph AFB may fall within accident potential zones or other base-proximity overlays that affect permitted uses, so confirming those restrictions is a necessary step before a property is placed on a written identification. Coordination for a Northeast San Antonio file typically covers:

  • Base-proximity overlay or accident potential zone confirmation for properties near Randolph AFB
  • Written identification meeting the unambiguous-description standard under Treasury Regulation 1.1031(k)-1
  • Lease abstract and tenant credit review for the specific property
  • Lender preflight against I-35 corridor industrial or Northeast retail pricing
  • Exchange agreement and assignment documents routed to the qualified intermediary before funding

This tracks documentation and timeline; it is not a substitute for the investor's own legal or tax review.

sections.heading: Watchouts for Northeast San Antonio Investors
sections.html: Base-proximity restrictions near Randolph AFB can limit certain uses or building heights, which is worth confirming early rather than discovering after a property has been identified. Industrial product along I-35 also competes directly with similar space in the Austin corridor, so investors should benchmark cap rates against both metros rather than assuming San Antonio pricing alone sets the ceiling.

faqs.q: What kind of commercial property is available in Northeast San Antonio?
faqs.a: A mix of retail centers along FM 78 and the northeast Loop 410 quadrant serving the Judson and Converse residential areas, plus light industrial and flex space along the I-35 corridor itself.

faqs.q: Does Randolph Air Force Base affect nearby property use restrictions?
faqs.a: Properties close to the base may fall within accident potential zones or other proximity overlays that limit certain uses or building heights, so this should be confirmed before finalizing a written identification on a nearby property.

faqs.q: Why does I-35 attract industrial tenants to this corridor?
faqs.a: Direct highway access lets logistics and light industrial operators serve both the San Antonio and Austin metros from a single Northeast San Antonio location, which is a meaningful draw given the growth in both cities.

faqs.q: Is Northeast San Antonio retail demand tied to the military?
faqs.a: Substantially, yes. Randolph AFB provides a stable population of military families and personnel that supports nearby retail and service tenants in a way that's less sensitive to broader economic cycles than purely civilian trade areas.

faqs.q: Should I compare Northeast San Antonio industrial pricing against Austin?
faqs.a: It's worth doing, since industrial product along I-35 here competes directly with similar space closer to Austin, and benchmarking against both metros gives a more accurate read on where cap rates should land.
